title: Rainwater and Dryland Agriculture—An Overview
abstract:
Management of rain water is the key to success in dryland agriculture as
most of the cultivated areas in India as well as in the entire world
depends on rainfed farming. An integrated approach for the management
of rain water consists of increasing moisture storage in the soil, optimum
use of this moisture through efficient cropping systems, harvesting a
portion of the runoff water for "life saving" irrigation, and reorientating
the concepts and organization of irrigation, including that from tanks, to
enhance productivity per unit of rain water.
publication: Symposium on Rainwater and Dryland Agriculture
